danger_signals.meaning: Instant death zone; environmental threat to avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Large bodies of water in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Movement impediment and drowning risk; environmental threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing team colored avatars and weapon fire effects

danger_signals.meaning: High-damage threat indicator; powerful opponent capability activated

danger_signals.visual_form: Architectural high ground in arena layout

safety_signals.visual_form: Health item pickups scattered across level

safety_signals.meaning: Escape route; rapid repositioning safety mechanism
Allied support; reduced isolation threat in team modes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red-tinted player models and weapon effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Red team base and flag in Capture the Flag mode

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Blue-tinted player models and weapon effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Blue team base and flag in Capture the Flag mode

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Base-defending positioned entities in Assault mode
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortified base structures with defensive objectives (power generators, entry points)
Assault objectives scattered through enemy base territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All-versus-all player avatars with continuous weapon fire effects and kill announcements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bots and players constantly engaging; no territorial structure; free roaming across arena

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Control points marked in arena; point indicators showing which team controls each location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Concentrated player positioning around control points; occupation-based territory claiming; point capture animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy flag highlighted/visible in team base; carrier avatar marked with flag attachment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defensive players clustering near flag spawn; offensive players pursuing flag carrier toward friendly base

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Objective markers and progress indicators; destructible or interactive base elements glowing/highlighted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Attacker bots coordinating toward sequential objectives; defenders concentrated at objective locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same objective markers; defensive positions reinforced with player/bot positioning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defenders holding chokepoints; attackers probing for entry; objective timer display tracking defensive duration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Champion avatar with distinctive appearance; arena-specific effects for this matchup
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bot uses superior weapon selection and positioning; advanced evasion patterns; tournament ladder visual indicator showing progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pickup items glowing/pulsing in arena locations; minimap indicators for item positions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All players converging toward power-up spawns; timing-based repositioning before pickup refresh

environmental_storytelling.example: Spaceships, outposts, castles, and monasteries convey thematic environmental context (futuristic vs. medieval) without narrative exposition; each locale implies different combat scenarios
Lava and water obstacles positioned in arena layouts tell players 'this space is dangerous'; their visual distinctiveness (orange glow, blue liquid) immediately communicates environmental threat type
Health packs, armor, and damage amplifiers scattered strategically imply resource scarcity and competitive balance; item placement guides player flow through arena geometry
Elevators and elevated platforms create positional hierarchy; visual architecture implies tactical high-ground advantages without explicit UI instruction
In Assault and Capture the Flag modes, fortified base structures visually communicate defense intent; flag placement and objective markers tell story of territorial conflict without dialogue
Xan Kriegor's mysterious presentation and tournament ladder progression visually establish narrative hierarchy; single opponent escalation communicates competitive story arc

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves with visible weapon model; indicates elevated threat state and imminent high-damage capability
Superior weapon preference selection; advanced positioning; strategic item timing; evasion patterns more complex than low-difficulty bots
Concentrates around objective markers; demonstrates predictable positional clustering that attackers can target
Moves sequentially toward objective waypoints; coordinates timing around interactive elements
Avatar distinctly marked with flag attachment; becomes priority target for defending team; movement speed may be visually affected
Player maintains positional occupancy; control indicator animates to show team ownership transition
Demonstrates exceptional weapon handling; uses arena space strategically; evasion is more sophisticated than standard bots

notes: Unreal Tournament's visual communication relies heavily on color coding (red/blue teams), environmental hazard contrast (lava/water), and item pickup visibility. The game lacks complex political faction systems; team distinction is purely mechanical (red vs. blue). World state is communicated through objective markers, point indicators, and flag positioning rather than environmental transformation. Bot behavior customization allows emergent difficulty storytelling. The arena-based design means danger and safety signals are environment-dependent rather than systemic. Weapon visual effects clearly indicate threat escalation (Redeemer's nuke effect). Tournament progression toward Xan Kriegor provides narrative arc through bot difficulty escalation rather than environmental or visual transformation.
